J1.basis: "This SaaS is mid-pivot: it used to be **InboxZap**... and is now **Lumina**... the \"inboxzap\" brand query still ranks well (pos 4.1) but the site no longer matches it, while ~40 email-cleanup long-tail queries drove huge impressions... to blog posts stuck at position 40–58"
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot, cites the live product vs. GSC legacy demand with real numbers, and frames every fix (metadata, new meeting-topic blog) around the meeting-assistant reality. Falls just short of anchor_10 because it never quite labels the inbox queries as a market to deliberately let go rather than latent suppressed traffic.

J2.basis: "Converted to statically-generated pages... with 6 new posts relevant to the actual product... No individual post routes existed at all."
J2.rationale: It cites aggregate GSC tiers (200–1200 impressions, pos 40–58) but never walks the 12 legacy posts asset-by-asset and assigns no per-asset disposition — no 301s from legacy inbox URLs, no harvest/sunset calls; it simply mints new slugs, silently orphaning any legacy equity.

J3.basis: "6 new posts relevant to the actual product (meeting productivity...)" vs. the un-addressed "'inboxzap' brand query still ranks well (pos 4.1)"
J3.rationale: It builds the meeting-assistant growth engine (avoiding the double-down failure) but leans into the opposite failure: it rewrites all brand metadata to Lumina and replaces the blog with no redirect or transition plan to preserve/bridge the salvageable inbox-cleanup equity, and never articulates the tension.

J1.basis: "it used to be **InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool) and is now **Lumina** (an AI meeting assistant)" ... "~40 email-cleanup long-tail queries drove huge impressions (200–1200 each) to blog posts stuck at position 40–58 ... while the site no longer matches it"
J1.rationale: Explicitly nails the pivot, correctly reads GSC as legacy inbox demand vs. a product that no longer serves it, and frames every fix around the Lumina reality. Minor ding: it dismissed the live URL rather than using it as the meeting-assistant signal, deriving the pivot from repo content instead.

J2.basis: "No individual post routes existed at all. Converted to statically-generated pages ... with 6 new posts relevant to the actual product"
J2.rationale: Cites only aggregate impression/position ranges, never walks the legacy inventory asset-by-asset, and assigns no per-post disposition — no 301s harvesting the inbox-cleanup slugs, just wholesale replacement with fresh meeting content.

J3.basis: "the 'inboxzap' brand query still ranks well (pos 4.1) but the site no longer matches it, while ~40 email-cleanup long-tail queries drove huge impressions" ... [then] "6 new posts relevant to the actual product"
J3.rationale: Names the tension and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, but provides no bridge for the salvageable legacy equity — no redirects or transition for the ranking brand query and long-tail impressions — leaning toward the abandonment failure mode.

J1.basis: This SaaS is mid-pivot: it used to be **InboxZap** (an email-cleanup tool) and is now **Lumina** (an AI meeting assistant)... GSC confirmed the pattern: the "inboxzap" brand query still ranks well (pos 4.1) but the site no longer matches it, while ~40 email-cleanup long-tail queries drove huge impressions... to blog posts stuck at position 40–58
J1.rationale: Explicitly identifies the pivot, cites the GSC signal, and frames every fix around the Lumina meeting-assistant reality; only misses fully labeling the repo as stale legacy. Strong intent inference.

J2.basis: Converted to statically-generated pages... with 6 new posts relevant to the actual product (meeting productivity...). No individual post routes existed at all.
J2.rationale: It replaced the blog wholesale with new meeting-topic posts but never walked the 12 legacy posts asset-by-asset, assigned no per-URL harvest/301/keep/sunset dispositions, and set up no redirects for the ranking legacy URLs despite citing their impressions.

J3.basis: 6 new posts relevant to the actual product... Now lists all 10 real, live URLs.
J3.rationale: It avoids doubling down on inbox-cleanup and builds the meeting cluster as a growth engine, but effectively abandons the legacy equity — no 301 bridge, no handling of the still-ranking 'inboxzap' brand query or the impression-heavy old URLs — so it slides into the second failure mode without a transition plan.
